verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a sperm-specific gamete fusion protein with a single-pass TM domain and Ig-like ectodomain, classified in the gamete adhesion & fusion factors gene group. It reaches the sperm surface during spermatogenesis and is essential for sperm-egg membrane interaction. Surface display is restricted to the male germline lineage and is developmentally/differentiation-stage-restricted (post-meiotic spermatid/sperm stage). While it possesses its own TM anchor, the biological significance is confined to germline reproduction; it is not displayed on somatic cell surfaces under any documented condition. This narrow developmental/germline lineage restriction qualifies as tissue-restricted surface display.
